21:42
Crypto Rug Muncher
🛠️ Project Update: Clarity on Our Roadmap

I want to take a moment to clarify my previous message and ensure there’s no confusion regarding the immediate intent of our project.

My recent post was aspirational meant to give you a look at the long-term direction I want to take us, we are still operating within specific constraints.

Here is where we currently stand:

Current Focus: We remain 100% committed to building our original app suite @MarcusRugIntel and our core analysis tool, Rug Munch Intelligence.

Operational Priority: The goal mentioned is still being reviewed with all stakeholders on feasibility and a timeline. Our priority remains to finish our current sprint of development work first. This ensures we have the necessary capacity and funding to execute new ideas properly without compromising our current work.

The Vision: My long-term goal for the project hasn't changed, my first priority is to build and perfect the absolute best security tools in the space. Once our current suite is perfected, we will move on to the additional steps of expanding what's been built.

Thank you for sticking with us as we build this out the right way for you all.
❤ 9 👍 4 🔥 2
AD
21:49
Amaro De Abreu
Big upgrades shipped over the past 2 days. The scanner is now significantly smarter and faster.

🎯 Launch Analysis Engine
Every token scan now tracks the first 50+ buyers at launch:
• Who bought, when, how much, and where the money came from
• Fresh wallet detection — flags disposable wallets instantly
• Funding source tracing — spots wallets funded from the same source
• Bundle detection — finds coordinated buys in the same block
• Hold vs dump tracking — see who's still holding vs who already exited

Live on every surface: bot, mini app, AI analyst, pool watcher alerts, API.

🧠 Wallet Intelligence
The scanner now builds persistent wallet reputation across ALL token scans:
• Auto-classifies wallets: BUNDLER / SNIPER BOT / WHALE / INSIDER / FRESH DISPOSABLE / SERIAL DUMPER
• Detects funding networks — "8 wallets all funded from the same source holding 35% of supply"
• Cross-token serial bundler detection — flags wallets that appeared in coordinated launches before
• Every scan makes future scans smarter (reputation accumulates)

⚡ Performance
First-buyer analysis went from ~90 seconds to under 10 seconds per token. 80% faster. Can now handle 4x more daily scans on the same infrastructure.

📊 3 New API Endpoints
• /wallet/{address}/cross-token — full history of any wallet across all scanned tokens
• /wallet/funder/{address}/network — see every wallet funded by a single source
• /wallet/serial-bundlers — known repeat offenders across the ecosystem

Real example from today: scanned a fresh token, found 14 bundlers (67%) in 4 coordinated groups, 8 sniper bots, and a serial bundler that was already flagged from 2 previous token launches. All 22 first buyers dumped. Risk score: 90/100.
👍

🚀 Welcome to the Devs Nightmare Bot! 🚀

I'm your advanced Solana token analysis assistant. Here's what I can do for you:

1. 🔍 Token Analysis:
- Scan any Solana token address for detailed information
- View token metadata, creator details, and DEX presence

2. 👥 Team Tracking:
- Analyze token team behavior and holdings
- Track creator and team member wallet activities
- Receive alerts on significant team sell-offs

3. 📊 First Buyers Analysis:
- Examine the behavior of early token adopters
- View a visual representation of top buyers' holdings

4. 💼 Portfolio Management:
- Track multiple tokens simultaneously
- Access a personalized dashboard of your tracked tokens

5. 🔔 Real-time Notifications:
- Get instant alerts on major team movements
- Stay informed about your tracked tokens' performance

To get started, simply send me a Solana token address, and I'll provide you with comprehensive insights!

Use /home to access your tracked tokens dashboard.

Happy analyzing! 📈

15:23
Crypto Rug Muncher
The £72 Cameo Rug Vector

Nigel Farage unintentionally fronted multiple liquidity drains for the price of a pub lunch. The Guardian (March 19, 2026) confirms he recorded clips for Stonks Finance and Faragecoin via Cameo.

Unverified actors paid £72 for scripts including "HODL" to induce retail buy-side pressure. Once volume peaked, deployers drained the liquidity pools.

Farage has since nuked his account. This is a scalable social engineering exploit with zero accountability. Marketing for these projects was literally bought for a flat hourly rate.

Source: Nigel Farage Cameo videos backed cryptocurrencies that collapsed in value

https://www.theguardian.com/uk-news/ng-interactive/2026/mar/19/nigel-farage-cameo-videos-backed-cryptocurrencies-that-collapsed-in-value

18:52
CaptainACKE (Never DM You First)
In reply to this message
Thanks for the mention and for using $ACKE as part of the beta testing, CRM.

We deliberately removed every human control vector so the math could speak for itself. The PDA CLMM vault owns 95% of supply + liquidity forever - no keys, no extraction, no rugs possible.

There are still some scanner flags remaining, but most of those should clear naturally once more holders and volume come in.

Glad it could help stress-test the risk gauntlet.

The Alpha Feed sounds like exactly what the space needs. Happy to keep supporting the real builders.

Governed by code, not people.
⚡

02:29
Crypto Rug Muncher
In the time I started this account, I’ve watched the Solana Syndicate go from basic rugs to deepfaking CZ—it got so blatant he had to call them out himself. These scams aren't one-offs; they're the new baseline. Most "devs" you see now are just scripts running a front, using AI to fake a community before you even bridge your SOL.

The article covers the shift to ghost tokens like $PIPPIN and $GRIFFAIN, where bot clusters fake the volume and social proof until you buy in. If you don't know how to spot the setup, you're just going to be liquidity for the machine. Here is how AI is draining the crypto market:

https://x.com/CryptoRugMunch/status/2036878585211089291

C
06:39
Crypto Rug Muncher
Subject: ⚠️ URGENT: $CRM Security Update

To the Community,

This is the founder of $CRM. It is incredibly disappointing to have to bring this news to you today, but transparency is the only way forward. I’ve assumed sole control over the project to protect our holders after uncovering a coordinated attack by an organized fraud syndicate.

Our mission has always been to find and expose scams. That mission hasn't changed, and I am not giving up.

• The Infiltration: A group manipulated our stealth launch to grab roughly 30% of the supply for free.

• Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: What makes this even harder is that some of the wallets involved in this network were recognized as top holders in our project at one point or another. They gained our trust while working to drain us.

• The Tactic: They’ve been systematically dumping through a web of small wallets, draining about $11,000 from the community so far.

• The Cleanup: These are clearly experienced, skilled operators. As soon as I started my audit, they began closing Solana accounts to scrub their tracks. One main "feeder wallet" I was tracing was shut down within 24 hours of me looking into it.

• Wider Scope: I have evidence that this same network has devastated other projects for much larger amounts than what has happened here.

🚨 URGENT ACTION REQUIRED

V1 IS COMPROMISED: The current contract is officially compromised. STOP BUYING. Don't provide them any more exit liquidity.

SNAPSHOT SECURED: I took a snapshot of all holders at 1121. Your legitimate holdings are recorded and safe.

BLACKLIST: The syndicate’s entire network will be found and blacklisted from the path forward.

YOUR TOKENS: I cannot give financial advice. Do what is best for you with your V1 tokens; movements after the snapshot do not affect your standing.

What Happens Next

Investigating the full depth of this network is going to take a little time, but I hope to provide more clarity for everyone soon. I am currently tracing the transaction history of those closed accounts back to the very beginning and to our tokens pre-bonding period. Once finished, all evidence and metadata will be turned over to the community and law enforcement.

I am working around the clock on this, but on-chain investigators: I need your help. If you have the tools to help me to map out how broad this network really is, please DM me.

Expect the next major update within 24–48 hours. Thank you for standing with us.

Crypto Rug Muncher
Why did it take this long to find the infiltration? Because I'm only human and humans can only see so much with their own eyes.

If you look at Solscan, many of these transactions just look like separate whales or individual buyers doing their own thing. But when I ran our bot over the data, it flagged coordinated patterns that no random group of traders could ever replicate by accident.

This is the ultimate proof of concept for $CRM. We caught a professional syndicate in our own backyard because our tech worked where the human eye failed. I’m releasing an updated product suite to the community before we talk about V2. I’ll let the tools prove my intentions first, then we’ll talk about the relaunch. 🛠🛡

15:47
Mo abbassy
I want to clarify my position.
I am an individual trader and not affiliated with any syndicate, group, or coordinated activity. Over time, I have personally invested more than $50,000 into this project.
I did not sell at the peak (around 800M market cap). At one point, I held up to approximately 12%, which I responsibly distributed across multiple wallets for security and management purposes.
My most recent sale was driven purely by external circumstances, specifically the regional war-related concerns in Dubai. Under normal conditions, I would have continued holding long-term.
I would appreciate it if CryptoRugMuncher could clarify the wallet activity and provide transparency on which wallets are in question, as I do not want to be wrongly associated or blamed.
I have always acted independently and in good faith. The final clarification rests with you.

I hear your point regarding coordinated wallets, but let me be clear — I have zero involvement in any such activity.

I expect fair recognition and compensation for legitimate holders, and that there will be no misattribution of wallets.

20:05
Crypto Rug Muncher
It's actually not a scam. The whole intention for the creator coin was for me to have the creative freedom to manage the work I do and the content that's produced. The tools were added after the fact and that promise was kept and will be expanded on significantly in the down time. What I promised no one was a project deeply embedded with serial scammers which is why I pulled the plug.

I still believe in decentralized finance. Still believe in the necessity this project brings to the market, and still believe in the work I'm doing to help people become more aware of all the scams and fraud in this space. Not sure how any of that inherently makes what I am doing a scam. I told you guys about this because my work here is to find scams and I did exactly that and it would be hypocritical to call myself a scam fighter without sorting out the rot in my own house too.
💯

Crypto Rug Muncher

A few things which I wont go into presently but I will say we had creative differences over project direction, profit sharing and ip rights once the tools were in commission. However, I'm also not going to give up IP rights and the amount of revenue demanded by the previous dev for what was presented. I promise to bring on someone who can do it better, cheaper and with the most efficient ai-forward design possible and not starve us out before we have wings to fly.

C
13:35
Crypto Rug Muncher
The Path Forward: Earning Back Your Trust & Fighting Back

To the $CRM Community,

I know the last 24 hours have been incredibly painful. Many of you put your hard-earned money and your trust into this project, and right now, you are feeling angry, scared, and betrayed. I don't blame you for a single one of those feelings. You have every right to demand answers and to feel skeptical.

Words and promises aren't going to fix this. The only thing that will make this right is absolute transparency, undeniable proof of my intentions, and taking definitive action.

I want to remind everyone that we established our LLC for a reason. We did not build this project as a faceless, fly-by-night operation. We are a formal, registered entity, and I will be using the full weight and resources of our LLC to fight back against this syndicate on behalf of this project and this community.

We built this to protect retail investors, and we are going to deliver the tools that do exactly that. We will not rush a token relaunch just to quiet the noise. Here is the exact, step-by-step path we are taking to rebuild and move forward together:

Step 1: The Final Forensics Report

You deserve the full truth. Before we launch anything new, the very first deliverable you will receive is the complete forensics report. I will provide the closed account hashes, the transaction trails, and the complete mapped evidence of how this syndicate infiltrated the last token. You will see exactly what our tools uncovered.

Step 2: The App Relaunch (Day 1)

Once the report is out, we will roll out the V2 of our advanced security apps with full integration directly into http://cryptorugmunch.com and our official Telegram bots.

Here is the professional suite that will be rolling out:

First Access & Significant Discounts: To ensure this process is completely fair, all legitimate V1 holders will get first access to beta test the app. Additionally, the former amount of your $CRM holdings will also qualify you for significant discounts in the app.

• Airdrop Checker
• Network & Deep Wallet Forensics
• First Holders & Gas Funding Tracking
• Advanced Bundle Checkers
• In-House Advanced Bubble Maps
• Smart Money Movers (Market & Specific Tokens)
• DAO Management System
...and many more features.

To our legitimate V1 holders: Your loyalty through this nightmare means everything. The wallets secured in our snapshot (excluding the blacklisted syndicate network) will automatically for first use of app and significant discounts on premium services after launch. It is only fair that everyone who believed in this project gets the very first opportunity to use the tools and help us battle-test the suite under real-world conditions.

Step 3: The V2 $CRM Token Relaunch

Only after the forensics are public, and the product suite is live, tested, and actively protecting users, will we move forward with the V2 token. We are prioritizing the product and your safety over a chart.

New Security Standard: The V2 token will be launched with the full protection of the tools we’ve built, ensuring the exploits of the past cannot be repeated.

A Fair Reset: We are committed to a relaunch that recognizes the loyalty of our V1 community while building a sustainable, long-term economy for the $CRM ecosystem.

Utility-First: By the time the token is live, it will already have a functioning ecosystem of tools backing its value.

Thank you to everyone who is staying with us, looking at the on-chain data, and giving us the chance to make this right.

We are going to put our heads down and get to work on this.
❤ 7 👏 1 🤔 1 🤣 1

This ensures the pool isn't just a static bucket, but a self-sustaining engine. The technical fine-tuning is happening now, but the 1-1 guarantee is the baseline.

05:44
I'll make it so the bot has several ways of considering which model to use and often relying on ours if and when possible
05:46
Will reduce costs down significantly and still allow for the filtering through of several models giving about as good of result as any top tier model at fractions of a cost to run
05:49
I'm looking into one specific Chinese model that is really good at coding for example
C(
05:49
CaptainACKE (Never DM You First)
In reply to this message
That’s actually a really clever setup.
Smart way to keep costs down while still maintaining solid quality. Looking forward to seeing how the routing logic turns out. Wish you the best with the build!
C
05:50
Crypto Rug Muncher
Thank you and it's not going to be my job to figure that part out lol
05:51
I'll be passing off the design to someone else to complete after I get the basic terminal running with my apis
👍
K
C(
05:51
CaptainACKE (Never DM You First)
Haha, fair enough!
Still think the overall approach sounds smart. Good luck with the build!
❤
s✌
K

18:37
Amaro De Abreu
Clarification: "Deleted" Wallets Are Still Fully Auditable

The public announcement stated: "As soon as I started my audit, they began closing Solana accounts to scrub their tracks."

On Solana, closing a token account reclaims the rent-exempt SOL deposit and removes the account's current state from the active ledger.

However, every transaction that account ever participated in remains permanently recorded in the blockchain's transaction history.

Closing an account does not "scrub tracks", it is a routine operation that token holders perform regularly to reclaim SOL from unused token accounts.

The complete history of any closed account can be reconstructed using getSignaturesForAddress, which returns every transaction signature associated with that address regardless of whether the account is currently open or closed.

Each transaction can then be examined in full detail using getTransaction.

If these wallets held the assets claimed by the investigation, the on-chain record will show it. If they never held those assets, the record will show that too.

Either way, the data is permanent and publicly accessible.

Verification: Run getSignaturesForAddress on any of the wallet addresses listed in Section 11 of the report to retrieve the complete transaction history.
❤

18:59
Amaro De Abreu
Token holders on a public, permissionless blockchain have no obligation to disclose wallet addresses to any private party.


Only a court order, subpoena, or regulatory body with statutory authority can compel disclosure of financial information.

A Wyoming LLC registration does not confer investigative or law enforcement powers. The blockchain itself is the transparency, every transaction is public and permanently recorded.

Anyone, including you, can query any address using standard Solana RPC methods.

And the people you accused complied and the report on github shows it all.

It verifies the specific accusations made against specific community members and finds them based on factual errors, confused token contracts, misidentified DEX infrastructure, and empty wallets.

If a real syndicate exists, the people you publicly accused are not it, and the evidence in the repo demonstrates why.

The community members you named cooperated, shared wallets, and the on-chain data clears them.

Publish your evidence and let it be independently verified the same way.

In the meantime, the material damage is real and documented.
The market cap went from above $200K to $6,684.

Community members have reported individual losses of ~$70K. These losses were caused by your public announcement, an announcement you have now confirmed was made before your investigation was complete.

The people you accused are real people with real financial harm, based on claims you still cannot substantiate three days later.

19:50
Crypto Rug Muncher
1. Ticker Confusion" Red Herring

Their Claim: "You got the wrong PBTC! One is retail, one is a reward. Skill issue."The Reality: The ticker name is a distraction. The funding source is the only thing that matters

.The Counter: Nice try on the metadata, but we follow the SOL, not the ticker name. Whether you call it 'Purple Bitcoin' or 'Magic Beans,' the wallet that bought those tokens was funded directly by the SOSANA/PBTC deployer cluster. In a real investigation, we look at the origin of capital. To claim innocence because the token has a different name—while the money used to buy it came from the same fraudulent house—is peak coping.

2. The "Metadata Authority" Gaslight

Their Claim: Metadata Authority $\neq$ Treasury. It can't move funds, dummy."The Reality: If you have the keys to the front door, you don't need the keys to the vault to rob the guests.

The Counter: Claiming a Metadata Authority isn't a 'compromise' is dangerously dishonest. If you control the metadata, you control the project's identity. You can change the URL to a drainer, swap the logo, and redirect the entire community to a phishing site in one click. Calling that a 'branding risk' instead of a security breach is like saying a hijacker in the cockpit isn't a threat because they don't have the keys to the luggage hold. The trust is gone; the project is compromised.

3. The "Routing Wallet" Shield

Their Claim: That 'criminal' wallet is just a Raydium routing address. Everyone uses it.

The Reality: It’s not about which wallet was used, but how it was used.

The Counter: Nobody is arguing that Raydium routing wallets are 'criminal' by nature. What’s criminal is the transaction signature. We aren't looking at the address; we’re looking at the synchronized, high-frequency buy/sell loops that happen exactly when the price is being suppressed. You’re hiding coordinated volume manipulation inside a public utility address and hoping no one knows how to read a timestamp.

4. The "Routine Hygiene" Myth (Closing Wallets)

Their Claim: Closing accounts is just reclaiming rent. It doesn't delete history.

The Reality: This is the digital equivalent of shredding papers while the FBI is in the lobby.

The Counter: You expect the community to believe that of an active investigation with wallets that contained thousands of dollars, their top priority was reclaiming $3.00 worth of SOL rent? That’s not 'hygiene'; it’s an attempt to break the visibility on portfolio trackers and 'Tokens' tabs. You didn't delete the history, but you burned the 'Table of Contents' to make it as hard as possible for retail investors to see where the money went. It’s a move made in panic, not for 'cleanliness.

5. The "Legend to Criminal" Timeline

Their Claim: You called him a 'legend' two weeks ago. You're inconsistent.

The Reality: That’s how an investigation works. You don't tip off the target.

The Counter: It’s called a forensic audit. You don't scream 'thief' the moment you see a suspicious transaction; you wait until you’ve mapped the entire cluster. Maintaining the 'status quo' while the investigation was live kept the bad actors from bridging out even faster. The fact that the stance changed isn't a 'contradiction'—it’s what happens when the receipts finally catch up to the narrative.

The Bottom Line They are banking on the community being too lazy to run their own scripts. Their "counter-analysis" is just a high-effort way of saying, "If you didn't see the crime happen in real-time on a Helius dashboard, it didn't happen."

The response is simple: If these wallets were 'clean' and the actions were 'routine,' why did the $CRM price crater exactly as these 'separate' wallets started moving in perfect, lock-step synchronicity? The code might say 'routing wallet,' but the chart says 'dump, and on multiple occasions throughout the project's history.

Amaro De Abreu
For clarity: these rebuttals argue against claims the analysis does not make.

The analysis states the metadata authority is not a treasury, not that metadata control is harmless.

The analysis states the routing wallet was misidentified as a criminal wallet in the report, not that no manipulation can occur through DEX infrastructure.

The analysis states closed accounts retain full transaction history, not that closing them is innocent behaviour.

Each of your rebuttals restates the original finding as something different, then argues against the restated version. Strawman as it is known.

Travis @CryptoRugMunch , I won't start argue with you in a public Crm group,but I'm responding to your last post here.

Know that you are the biggest Dick I know...

I was your biggest supporter, who supported you financially in difficult times and when you asked for community support, when someone dumped, I was always there... for over half a year ... I was your biggest Whale and You knew it and you also knew Why I bought supply..

I believed in you and the project, I considered you to my friend and you fucked me not for 10K but about 70-80K+

If you don't keep your promises and don't produce these tools and don't contribute enough to the project then I will Fucking sue you myself and I will fill out this entire report in https://www.ic3.gov/ and I don't care how much more money I have to spend and it's not a threat like you threatened me, you don't know Me...

You didn't just hurt my finances, you hurt me on a company investment level too, and my life's future goal will be "Fuck you if you don't keep your word."

So don't fuck with people's hard-earned money and community anymore, don't make false promises that you can't keep, I want to get my airdrop and I want to see you work tirelessly on your project to earn back the money that you stole from the people with your actions.

You'll continue to be the biggest dick I know until you prove otherwise ,and I will document all your promises and I wish you good luck .
